name: Roy Hamilton
abstract: Roy Hamilton (April 16, 1929 – July 20, 1969) was an American singer. By combining semi-classical technique with traditional black gospel feeling, he brought soul to Great American Songbook singing.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for C.O.R.E. and Virginia - Jamaica Student Help Project

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: Right away, I thought it was Roy Hamilton. So, I said, 'Well, send him up.' Then, when he came up I said, 'What is his first name?' ... He said, 'Lionel!' I said, 'What!' He got the name wrong.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: And on that show it was Perry Como and Nat Cole, Roy Hamilton, Ralph Marterie band, Peg Leg Bates, Sarah Vaughn, and a cast of thousands, I guess you could say.

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: Roy Hamilton was one of the stars, and Billy Williams’ quartet. Because they had been on “Toast of the Town” and so they were the stars.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Ousley interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Also I first saw Roy Hamilton. Remember him, Roy Hamilton? Fine, fine vocalist.
